You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A seated male in a white sleeveless shirt extends his leg while smiling. A standing male in a white shirt and red shorts reads a paper. Another male in a green shirt leans over the extended leg, holding a small jar near the ankle. A wooden table edge becomes visible in the foreground right.</think><action>silent</action>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text:
[0-2] A seated male in a white sleeveless shirt extends his leg while smiling. A standing male in a white shirt and red shorts reads a paper, while another male in a green shirt leans over holding a jar near the ankle. A wooden table edge becomes visible in the foreground right.
[2-8] The male in the green shirt spreads a brown substance onto the leg using a wooden stick. The standing male in red shorts holds a white rectangular strip poised above the shin. The camera zooms in on the leg as the substance is applied further up.
[8-12] The camera pans upward abruptly to the seated male's upper body. He wears a white sleeveless shirt with an orange 'TEXAS' graphic and looks downward. A dark wooden door and framed portraits are visible behind him.
[12-14] The view shifts back down to the extended leg. The individual in red shorts bends over, holding the white strip above the waxed area, while hands with the jar and stick remain near the foot.

[14-22] A person in a white shirt and red shorts places a white strip onto a hairy shin resting on a wooden chair. They use both hands to press and smooth the paper firmly against the leg. The camera angle stays high, focusing on the top of the blonde individual's head as they secure the strip, while another person holds a wax jar nearby.
[22-28] The individual adjusts their hands from flat pressing to gripping the edges of the white strip. They shift their hands lower, pressing firmly near the ankle area to secure the wax strip against the shin. The leg remains extended on the wooden chair, with a wooden table visible on the right, as the person continues to smooth the paper.

recent_thinks: [28-30] The individual in the white shirt and red shorts continues pressing the white strip onto the lower leg, smoothing it down near the ankle. The leg remains extended on the wooden chair, with the blonde head visible above. Another person holds a jar to the right.

[30-32] The person in the white shirt and red shorts rubs the white strip firmly against the lower leg to secure it. Their hands smooth the paper down from the shin towards the ankle. The leg stays extended on the wooden chair while another person holds a wax jar nearby.

[32-34] The person in the white shirt and red shorts stops rubbing the strip and instead grips the loose edge of the white paper with both hands. The strip is firmly pressed against the hairy shin resting on the wooden chair. Another individual holds a wax jar to the right.

[34-36] The individual in the white shirt and red shorts presses both hands flat against the white strip on the shin, smoothing the paper firmly down to secure it. The leg stays extended on the wooden chair while another individual holds a jar to the right. The blonde head is visible above the leg.

[36-38] The person in the white shirt and red shorts presses both hands flat against the white strip on the shin, smoothing the paper firmly down to secure it. The leg stays extended on the wooden chair while another individual holds a jar to the right. The blonde head is visible above the leg.

[38-40] The person in the white shirt and red shorts presses both hands flat against the white strip on the shin, smoothing the paper firmly down to secure it. The leg stays extended on the wooden chair while another individual holds a jar to the right. The blonde head is visible above the leg.

samples.output: <think>The person in the white shirt and red shorts presses both hands flat against the white strip on the shin, smoothing the paper firmly down to secure it. The leg stays extended on the wooden chair while another individual holds a jar to the right. The blonde head is visible above the leg.</think><action>silent</action>
